step: Inspect `codex-dashboard/server.js` and `codex-dashboard/index.html` to identify the exact task/execution fields already exposed for active work, then document the minimal source-of-truth fields needed to show current owner, execution state, attempt, and latest progress note for each active task.

task_board_cards.frontend_use: Approved and historical task cards already render execution and recent activity details from `tasks[]`.
task_board_cards.gap: The board does not currently render an explicit owner or worker field.

system_status_card.frontend_use: The runtime status card shows one current task and one status note, but not per-task ownership.

live_work_panel_payload.frontend_state: The Live Work strip markup exists in `index.html`, but this payload is not currently consumed by the frontend script.

active_tasks_endpoint.frontend_state: This endpoint is currently unused by `index.html`.

minimal_source_of_truth_fields.scope_rule: Treat a task as active work when `tasks[].execution.state` is `running` or `retrying`.

primary_fields.name: worker_owner
primary_fields.why: This mirrors the live-work backend chain: `activeTaskOwnership(...)` resolves explicit owner and worker fields first, then falls back to the provider derived in `buildLiveWorkPanel()` from `execution.provider`, `execution_provider`, or `provider_selection.selected`.

primary_fields.name: worker_state
primary_fields.why: This is already the canonical active/inactive discriminator across the dashboard and shell helpers.

primary_fields.name: attempt
primary_fields.why: The board already renders attempt badges from these fields.

primary_fields.name: latest_progress_note
primary_fields.why: This matches `activeTaskWorkLabel(...)`: prefer explicit current-step fields, then the next planned step from `execution_context.plan_steps[completed_steps]`, then deterministic retry or in-progress labels, and only then use server-exposed history notes for richer task-board context.

notes: The existing `/api/metrics.live_work_panel.items[]` payload is suitable for a compact strip, but not rich enough by itself for task-card level execution history.
The existing `/api/active-tasks` payload exposes state and attempt, but it does not expose owner or latest progress note.
